content: I'm feeling really inspired about my work right now. I've been working on some sustainable design projects and I'm excited to see them come to life.
That's great to hear, Jamie! Sustainable design is such an important field, and it's wonderful that you're passionate about it. What kind of projects have you been working on?
I've been working on a few different things, including a green roof project for a new building in Pittsburgh. It's been a challenge, but it's going to be really rewarding to see it finished.
That sounds like a fantastic project. I'm sure it will make a big impact on the environment and the community. Have you been working with any other architects or designers on the project?
I have, and it's been great to collaborate with them. We've been sharing ideas and expertise, and it's been really helpful to have a team behind me.
I've also been trying to balance my work and personal life better. I've been training for a triathlon and it's been really helpful to have something to focus on outside of work.
That's great that you're prioritizing your personal life and finding time for the things you enjoy. Triathlon training is no easy feat, though! How's your training going?

It's going well, thanks for asking. I've been putting in a lot of hours, but it's been worth it. I've also been trying to get out and enjoy the city more, whether it's going to a Penguins game or checking out a new restaurant.
Pittsburgh is a great city, there's always something to do. Have you tried any new restaurants lately that you'd recommend?
Actually, I have. I went to a great Italian place in the Strip District last weekend. The pasta was homemade and it was amazing.
That sounds delicious. I'll have to keep that in mind for future recommendations. What's next for you in terms of work projects? Do you have any big plans or goals?
I do, actually. I'm thinking about starting my own sustainable design firm, maybe in the next year or so. It's a big goal, but I think it's achievable.
That's exciting! Starting your own firm will be a challenge, but it's great that you're thinking ahead and planning for your future. You'll have to keep me updated on your progress.
I will, thanks for the support. I'm also thinking about getting a new car, maybe a vintage one. I've always loved old cars and I think it would be a fun project to restore one.
That sounds like a fun project. Restoring a vintage car can be a lot of work, but it's a great way to learn new skills and have a unique vehicle.
I'm thinking about looking for a 1969 Mustang, that's always been my dream car. And I'm also thinking about taking up salsa dancing, I've always loved the music and the energy of the dance.
That sounds like a great idea, Jamie. You should definitely go for it and pursue your passions, whether it's sustainable design, vintage cars, or salsa dancing.
